name: Kellogg Midwest
description_short: Federal credit union in La Vista, NE with $54.9M in assets, 2,567 members, and 2 branches offering checking, savings, and loan products since 1946.
description_long:
Kellogg Midwest Federal Credit Union is a community-based federal credit union established in 1946, making it an 80-year-old financial institution serving the La Vista, Nebraska area. The organization is a member-owned, not-for-profit entity regulated by the National Credit Union Administration (NCUA) with charter number 5286, operating under a community membership model open to urban or rural members outside low-income designations.

The credit union offers a range of traditional banking services including checking and savings accounts, ATM/debit card programs, real estate loans ($24.2M in portfolio), unsecured personal loans ($1.1M outstanding), and other loan products totaling $32.9M in loans. They maintain $42M in member shares and deposits across various account types including share drafts and regular savings accounts, with total assets of $54.9 million as of March 2025. The organization operates 2 branch locations and employs 10 full and part-time staff members.

Kellogg Midwest distinguishes itself as a small, locally-focused credit union with deep community roots spanning eight decades. Their leadership structure includes Jennifer Warnsing as CEO/Manager and Ron Kmiecik as Chairperson. The institution maintains a conservative financial profile with substantial equity capital ($12.1M) relative to assets, indicating financial stability. They reported net income of $89,591 for the most recent period and maintain no secondary debt obligations, suggesting a straightforward capital structure.

As a smaller regional credit union, Kellogg Midwest serves a niche membership base of approximately 2,567 members. The institution is not an FHLB member and carries membership restrictions limited to community membership categories. For consumers seeking basic deposit and lending services with local oversight in the Omaha metro area, this credit union offers an alternative to larger financial institutions, though the limited branch network and smaller asset base mean fewer digital amenities or specialized products compared to major credit unions.
services: Checking accounts (share drafts with $8.95M in deposits)
Savings accounts (regular shares with $19.9M in deposits)
Money market and other deposit products ($13.2M in alternative shares/deposits)
Real estate loans and mortgages ($24.2M portfolio)
Other installment and consumer loans ($7.6M portfolio)
Loan services with credit loss allowance management
pros: 80-year operating history demonstrates long-term stability and community commitment since 1946
Strong equity-to-asset ratio of 22% ($12.1M capital on $54.9M assets) indicates solid financial health
Member-owned, not-for-profit structure with no secondary debt obligations or complex capital arrangements
NCUA federal insurance protection on deposits up to $250,000 per account category
Personal loan products available with real estate, unsecured, and other loan options totaling $32.9M
Accessible local leadership with named CEO and board chairperson directly accountable to membership
Low operating expense ratio suggesting efficient management and competitive fee structure potential
cons: Only 2 branch locations limits physical access and service convenience compared to larger credit unions
Small membership base (2,567 members) may result in limited product innovation or digital banking features
$54.9M in total assets is significantly smaller than regional competitors, constraining loan capacity and special services
No FHLB membership reduces funding flexibility and may impact competitive loan rates
Limited public information about mobile banking, online services, or digital-first offerings on the provided website data
best_for: Local La Vista and Omaha area residents seeking community-based banking with personal service and local decision-making
Primary residence homebuyers needing real estate financing with local credit union oversight and relationship banking
Small business owners or self-employed individuals within the membership eligibility criteria seeking personal or business lending
Conservative savers prioritizing NCUA insurance protection and member-ownership structure over extensive branch networks
diagnosis: Kellogg Midwest Federal Credit Union is best suited for established community members in the La Vista/Omaha area seeking straightforward deposit accounts, real estate loans, or personal loans with local service and member-ownership benefits. The primary caveat is the limited branch network (2 locations) and smaller asset base, which may restrict access to specialized products, competitive rates on larger loan amounts, or advanced digital banking features compared to regional or national credit unions.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
